meta.title: Defence Exam Vocab Bank
meta.pyq_note: pyq field is null if no MCQ question format was found in PYQ sources. correct_index is 0-based.

vocab_bank.meaning: The process of joining two or more things together to form a single entity
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The movie is about fusion of old and contemporary ideas.

pyq.question: The movie is about fusion of old and contemporary ideas. Choose the word OPPOSITE in meaning to the underlined word.

vocab_bank.meaning: The ability to do something successfully or efficiently
vocab_bank.example_sentence: Each child develops his/her competency based on the contexts and the inputs for learning.

pyq.question: Each child develops his/her competency based on the contexts and the inputs for learning. Choose the word NEAREST in meaning.

vocab_bank.meaning: The condition or state of being active; the execution of tasks or work
vocab_bank.example_sentence: There should have been much activity for the cultivation of the physical sciences.

pyq.question: Select the most appropriate antonym for the underlined word: there should have been much activity for the cultivation of the physical sciences.

vocab_bank.meaning: To become less intense or widespread; to reduce or diminish
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The storm gradually began to abate after midnight.

vocab_bank.meaning: Experienced or present to the maximum degree; completely without pride or dignity
vocab_bank.example_sentence: Millions of people are living in abject poverty.

vocab_bank.meaning: To solemnly renounce a belief, cause, or claim
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He abjured his allegiance to the former regime.

vocab_bank.meaning: Bitterness or ill feeling; harsh or biting sharpness in manner or speech
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The dispute was settled without too much acrimony.

vocab_bank.meaning: Refusing to be persuaded or to change one's mind; unshakeably firm
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He was adamant that he had not made a mistake.

vocab_bank.meaning: To warn or reprimand someone firmly; to advise against certain actions
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The teacher admonished the students for their careless work.

vocab_bank.meaning: A difficult or unpleasant situation; a state of misfortune or hardship
vocab_bank.example_sentence: She remained cheerful in the face of great adversity.

vocab_bank.meaning: To diminish or put at rest (fear, suspicion, or worry); to soothe
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The doctor tried to allay her fears about the operation.

vocab_bank.meaning: To make (suffering or a problem) less severe; to ease or lessen
vocab_bank.example_sentence: Drugs can alleviate the pain of patients.

vocab_bank.meaning: A deep-seated feeling of dislike; strong aversion or hostility
vocab_bank.example_sentence: His antipathy towards his opponents was well known.

vocab_bank.meaning: Lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern; indifference
vocab_bank.example_sentence: Voter apathy was blamed for the low election turnout.

vocab_bank.meaning: The willingness to take bold risks; rude or arrogant behaviour
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He had the audacity to tell me I was wasting his time.

vocab_bank.meaning: Of undisputed origin; genuine and trustworthy
vocab_bank.example_sentence: We need to check whether this painting is authentic.

vocab_bank.meaning: To perplex or confuse someone totally; to hinder or restrain
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The question completely baffled him.

vocab_bank.meaning: The quality of being well-meaning and kindly; goodwill toward others
vocab_bank.example_sentence: His benevolence towards the poor was renowned throughout the city.

vocab_bank.meaning: Showing or having an insensitive and cruel disregard for others
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He was callous about the suffering of others.

vocab_bank.meaning: An event causing great and often sudden damage or distress; a disaster
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The flood was the worst calamity to strike the region in a century.

vocab_bank.meaning: The making of false and defamatory statements about someone; slander
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He was a victim of calumny spread by his political rivals.

vocab_bank.meaning: To attract and hold the interest and attention of; to enchant
vocab_bank.example_sentence: Her performance captivated the audience from start to finish.

vocab_bank.meaning: Sympathetic pity and concern for the sufferings or misfortunes of others
vocab_bank.example_sentence: She had great compassion for homeless people.

vocab_bank.meaning: Standing out so as to be clearly visible; attracting notice or attention
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He was conspicuous by his absence at the ceremony.

vocab_bank.meaning: The feeling that a person or thing is worthless or deserving scorn
vocab_bank.example_sentence: His contempt for the law was evident in his actions.

vocab_bank.meaning: Subject to debate, dispute, or uncertainty; open to discussion
vocab_bank.example_sentence: Whether the policy was effective remains a moot point.

vocab_bank.meaning: To recognize or find out; to perceive or identify something with difficulty
vocab_bank.example_sentence: It was difficult to discern any pattern in the data.

vocab_bank.meaning: Deceit used in order to achieve one's goal; a trick or scheme
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He obtained entry to the building by subterfuge.

vocab_bank.meaning: To approach and address someone boldly or aggressively
vocab_bank.example_sentence: A beggar accosted them as they walked through the park.

vocab_bank.meaning: To express passionate grief or sorrow about; to mourn
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He lamented the decline of traditional values in society.

vocab_bank.meaning: In a very unhappy or unfortunate state; of very poor quality
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The wretched prisoners were kept in cramped conditions.

vocab_bank.meaning: The condition of having been deprived of something; grief after the death of a loved one
vocab_bank.example_sentence: She took time off work following the bereavement in her family.

vocab_bank.meaning: Excessive pride or self-confidence; arrogance that leads to one's downfall
vocab_bank.example_sentence: His downfall was caused by sheer hubris.

vocab_bank.meaning: Careful and circumspect in one's speech or actions; tactful
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He was discreet about his financial dealings.

vocab_bank.meaning: To make a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling worse
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The drought exacerbated the existing food shortage.

vocab_bank.meaning: To suggest or call attention to indirectly; to hint at
vocab_bank.example_sentence: She alluded to problems in her department without being specific.

vocab_bank.meaning: To escape or avoid by speed, cleverness, or trickery; to evade
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The suspect managed to elude the police for weeks.

vocab_bank.meaning: A procession or parade of people walking, on horseback, or riding in vehicles
vocab_bank.example_sentence: A cavalcade of military vehicles passed through the main street.

vocab_bank.meaning: The killing of a whole group of people or animals; total destruction
vocab_bank.example_sentence: Measures were taken for the extermination of the invasive pest species.

vocab_bank.meaning: Failing to produce the intended result; fruitless or ineffective
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He made an abortive attempt to escape from the prison.

vocab_bank.meaning: To declare someone free from guilt, obligation, or punishment
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The court absolved him of all charges.

vocab_bank.meaning: An official agreement or settlement; harmony of opinion or feeling
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The two nations signed a peace accord.

vocab_bank.meaning: To make something physically stronger or more solid; to combine into one
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The company decided to consolidate its operations under one brand.

vocab_bank.meaning: To refuse to accept; to deny the truth or validity of something
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The government repudiated the claims made by the opposition.

vocab_bank.meaning: Truthful and straightforward; frank and honest
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He gave a candid assessment of the team's performance.

vocab_bank.meaning: Having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The pernicious influence of poor discipline can destroy an army unit.

vocab_bank.meaning: Tending to talk a great deal; excessively talkative
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He was too loquacious for the serious tone of the military briefing.

vocab_bank.meaning: To ask for or try to obtain something from someone; to appeal or request
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The NGO solicited funds from the public for disaster relief.

vocab_bank.meaning: Holding firmly to a purpose, belief, or position; persistent
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The soldier's tenacious spirit helped him survive in adverse conditions.

vocab_bank.meaning: To officially absolve someone from blame or criminal charges
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He was exonerated by the court after new evidence came to light.

vocab_bank.meaning: To delay or prevent by obstructing; to hinder progress
vocab_bank.example_sentence: Logistical problems impeded the army's advance.

vocab_bank.meaning: Very generous or forgiving, especially towards a rival or less powerful person
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The magnanimous general spared the lives of enemy soldiers who surrendered.

vocab_bank.meaning: Not easily upset or excited; calm and peaceful in nature
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The placid surface of the lake reflected the mountains.

vocab_bank.meaning: Liable to change rapidly and unpredictably; highly unstable
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The political situation in the region remained volatile.

vocab_bank.meaning: To regard with great respect; to revere
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The nation venerates the memory of its fallen soldiers.

vocab_bank.meaning: Excessively obedient or attentive in an ingratiating or servile manner
vocab_bank.example_sentence: His obsequious behaviour towards superiors was widely noticed.

vocab_bank.meaning: Fearless; adventurous; resolutely courageous
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The intrepid soldier crossed enemy lines alone.

vocab_bank.meaning: Feeling or appearing casually calm and relaxed; not anxious or excited
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He appeared nonchalant about the results despite weeks of hard preparation.

vocab_bank.meaning: Suffering from extreme poverty; impoverished
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The government launched welfare schemes for indigent farmers.

vocab_bank.meaning: Weak or faint from illness or fatigue; lacking energy or vitality
vocab_bank.example_sentence: After the long march, the troops were too languid to celebrate.

vocab_bank.meaning: Having a particular habit, activity, or interest that is firmly established and unlikely to change
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He was an inveterate liar who could not be trusted.

vocab_bank.meaning: Severe or strict in manner or attitude; simple and plain without luxury
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The army's austere training regime prepared soldiers for harsh conditions.

vocab_bank.meaning: Using more words than are needed; wordy
vocab_bank.example_sentence: His verbose reports made it difficult to extract key information.

vocab_bank.meaning: To make someone anxious or unsettled; to disturb mentally
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The soldier remained composed and refused to be perturbed by threats.

vocab_bank.meaning: Bitterness or resentfulness, especially when long-standing
vocab_bank.example_sentence: There was deep rancour between the two rival factions.

vocab_bank.meaning: Not having any serious purpose or value; carefree and not serious
vocab_bank.example_sentence: He was reprimanded for making frivolous remarks during the briefing.

vocab_bank.meaning: Having or showing an ability to accurately assess situations and turn this to one's advantage; shrewd
vocab_bank.example_sentence: His astute judgement made him a respected military commander.

vocab_bank.meaning: Lasting only for a short time; temporary; brief
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The pain was transient and disappeared after a few hours.

vocab_bank.meaning: Reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The colonel was known to be taciturn, speaking only when necessary.

vocab_bank.meaning: Unwilling to spend money or use resources; excessively frugal
vocab_bank.example_sentence: His parsimonious nature meant he rarely contributed to shared expenses.

vocab_bank.meaning: Having or showing care and conscientiousness in one's work or duties
vocab_bank.example_sentence: A diligent officer always ensures his men are well prepared.

vocab_bank.meaning: Deceitful and untrustworthy; guilty of betrayal; treacherous
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The perfidious act of the spy endangered the entire mission.

vocab_bank.meaning: Acting or done quickly without thought or care; rash; hasty
vocab_bank.example_sentence: His impetuous decision led to unnecessary casualties on the battlefield.

vocab_bank.meaning: Having or showing keen mental discernment and good judgement; wise
vocab_bank.example_sentence: The sagacious general devised a plan that outsmarted the enemy.

vocab_bank.meaning: To put into a situation in which there is a risk of loss, harm, or failure
vocab_bank.hindi_meaning: खतरे में डालना / जोखिम में डालना
vocab_bank.example_sentence: Leaking classified information could jeopardize national security.
